---
title: "Exercise 4D: Build a RAG Chatbot"
subtitle: "MGMT 675: Generative AI for Finance"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \PassOptionsToPackage{colorlinks=true,urlcolor=blue,linkcolor=blue}{hyperref}
---

Build a RAG chatbot that answers questions about a corporate annual report.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Background.}
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) grounds an LLM's answers in actual documents. The pipeline has three stages: (1) chunk and embed a document into a vector database, (2) embed the user's query and retrieve the most relevant chunks, and (3) pass the retrieved chunks plus the query to an LLM for a grounded answer.

You will use an OpenRouter API key with a free model for the LLM. Sign up at \href{https://openrouter.ai}{openrouter.ai} and copy your API key.  Free models include \texttt{google/gemma-3-1b-it:free}, \texttt{meta-llama/llama-4-scout:free}, and others listed on the OpenRouter site.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Requirements.}
Ask Claude Code to build a Python app with the following features:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item \textbf{Document loading:} load a corporate 10-K or annual report PDF (e.g., download one from the SEC's EDGAR system).
  \item \textbf{Chunking and embedding:} split the document into chunks and embed them using \texttt{sentence-transformers} (or a similar embedding library). Store the embeddings in a local vector database such as ChromaDB or FAISS.
  \item \textbf{Query interface:} a chat-style interface (terminal or web) where the user types a question, the system retrieves the most relevant chunks, and passes them as context to the LLM.
  \item \textbf{LLM integration:} use the OpenRouter API (which is compatible with the OpenAI Python SDK) to generate answers. Example setup:
\begin{verbatim}
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
    api_key="your-openrouter-key",
)
\end{verbatim}
  \item \textbf{Source citations:} each answer should indicate which chunk(s) were used as context.
\end{enumerate}

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Suggested questions to test.}

\begin{itemize}
  \item What was total revenue in the most recent fiscal year?
  \item What are the main risk factors related to competition?
  \item Summarize management's outlook for the coming year.
  \item How much did the company spend on research and development?
\end{itemize}

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Deliverables.}

\begin{itemize}
  \item Code (\texttt{4D-Code.zip}) --- your complete RAG app
  \item A document (\texttt{4D-QA.pdf}) containing at least 5 question-answer pairs from your chatbot, with your assessment of each answer: was it grounded in the document? Accurate? Any hallucinations?
\end{itemize}
